title: podman run --dns-search : perform basic input validation?
body:
docker performs basic input validation on the argument to `--dns-search`. Should podman?
```
# for i in bad/search -example exam..pl $(printf 'X%.s' {1..300});do docker run --dns-search $i --rm fedora cat /etc/resolv.conf;done
invalid argument "bad/search" for --dns-search: bad/search is not a valid domain
See 'docker run --help'.
invalid argument "-example" for --dns-search: -example is not a valid domain
See 'docker run --help'.
invalid argument "exam..pl" for --dns-search: exam..pl is not a valid domain
See 'docker run --help'.
invalid argument "" for --dns-search:  is not a valid domain
See 'docker run --help'.
```

comments.body: If we can locate the docker parsing code that handles this, it should be easy to reuse for parsing our input.
